types.name: Genre
types.description: Genres for different media types. Follows format: TYPE_GENRE_SUBGENRE

types.name: ContentRating
types.description: Content rating for different media types. Follows format: TYPE_RATING

types.name: ContentRole
types.description: Content roles for different media types. Follows format: TYPE_ROLE_SUBROLE

types.name: SearchContentType
types.description: Types of media available on Taddy

types.name: TaddyType
types.description: Types of media available on Taddy

types.name: InternalSeriesType
types.description: Types of internal series available on Taddy

fields.name: uuid
fields.description: Taddy's unique identifier (an uuid)

fields.name: datePublished
fields.description: Date when the podcast was published (Epoch time in seconds)

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name (title) for a podcast

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description for a podcast

args.name: shouldStripHtmlTags
args.description: (Optional) Option to remove the html tags from the description or leave the description as is (which may include html tags). Default is false (leave description as is).

fields.name: descriptionLinks
fields.description: Extract all links from within the description.

fields.name: imageUrl
fields.description: The cover art for a podcast

fields.name: itunesId
fields.description: itunesId for the podcast

fields.name: hash
fields.description: A hash of all podcast details. It may be useful for you to save this property in your database and compare it to know if any podcast details have updated since the last time you checked

fields.name: childrenHash
fields.description: A hash of all episode details. It may be useful for you to save this property in your database and compare it to know if there are any new or updated episodes since the last time you checked

fields.name: episodes
fields.description: A list of episodes for this podcast

args.name: sortOrder
args.description: (Optional) Returns episodes based on SortOrder. Default is LATEST (newest episodes first), another option is OLDEST (oldest episodes first), and another option is SEARCH (pass in the property searchTerm) to filter for episodes by title or description.

args.name: page
args.description: (Optional) Taddy paginates the results returned. Default is 1, Max value allowed is 1000

args.name: limitPerPage
args.description: (Optional) Return up to this number of episodes. Default is 10, Max value allowed is 25 results per page

args.name: searchTerm
args.description: (Optional) Only to be used when sortOrder is SEARCH. Filters through the title & description of episodes for the searchTerm

args.name: includeRemovedEpisodes
args.description: (Optional) The option to show episodes that were once on the RSS feed but have now been removed. Default is false (do not include removed episodes)

fields.name: totalEpisodesCount
fields.description: The number of episodes for this podcast

args.name: includeRemovedEpisodes
args.description: (Optional) Option to include episodes that were once on the RSS feed but have now been removed. Default is false (do not include removed episodes)

fields.name: genres
fields.description: A podcast can belong to multiple genres but they are listed in order of importance. Limit of 5 genres per podcast

fields.name: itunesInfo
fields.description: Additional info from itunes on the podcast

fields.name: seriesType
fields.description: Podcast type (serial or episodic)

fields.name: language
fields.description: Language spoken on the podcast

fields.name: contentType
fields.description: Podcast's Content Type (Is the podcast primarily an Audio or Video Podcast)

fields.name: isExplicitContent
fields.description: Boolean for if the podcast contain's explicit content

fields.name: copyright
fields.description: Copyright details for the podcast

fields.name: websiteUrl
fields.description: The podcast's website

fields.name: rssUrl
fields.description: Url for the podcast's RSS feed

fields.name: rssOwnerName
fields.description: Name to use for contacting the owner of this podcast feed

fields.name: rssOwnerPublicEmail
fields.description: Email to use for contacting the owner of this podcast feed

fields.name: authorName
fields.description: Name of the Podcast creator (the podcast creator and the owner of the podcast feed can be different)

fields.name: feedRefreshDetails
fields.description: Details on how often the podcast is checked for new episodes

fields.name: taddyTranscribeStatus
fields.description: Whether the podcast is being automatically transcribed by our API

fields.name: popularityRank
fields.description: The popularity of the podcast. ex) TOP_200, TOP_1000 etc

fields.name: persons
fields.description: People listed on the podcast including their roles (Host, Guest, etc)

fields.name: isCompleted
fields.description: If the podcast is finished / complete

fields.name: isComplete
fields.description: (Old version) If the podcast is finished / complete

fields.name: isBlocked
fields.description: If the content has violated Taddy's distribution policies for illegal or harmful content it will be blocked from getting any updates

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.description: The url of the person's image

fields.description: (Experimental) Whether the person has been extracted automatically from transcript

fields.name: transcript
fields.description: Downloads the transcript, parses it and returns an array of text in paragraphs.

args.name: useOnDemandCreditsIfNeeded
args.description: (Optional) If the transcript is not available, use on-demand transcription. Default is true. On-demand transcription takes ~10 seconds for every 1 hour of audio transcribed. Also, consider using getEpisodeTranscript to offload getting the transcript to another query.

fields.name: transcriptWithSpeakersAndTimecodes
fields.description: Download the transcript, parses it and return an array of transcript items (which includes text, speakers and timecodes)

args.name: style
args.description: (Optional) Style option for transcript. Default is PARAGRAPH

args.name: useOnDemandCreditsIfNeeded
args.description: (Optional) If the transcript is not available, use on-demand transcription. Default is true. On-demand transcription takes ~10 seconds for every 1 hour of audio transcribed. Also, consider using getEpisodeTranscript to offload getting the transcript to another query.

fields.description: The start timecode of the transcript item in milliseconds

fields.description: The end timecode of the transcript item in milliseconds

types.name: FeedRefreshPriority
types.description: Different categories for how often a feed is refreshed

enumValues.name: WEBSUB
enumValues.description: Feed checked immediately (within 30 mins) after it has been updated (via a Websub notification)

enumValues.name: HIGH
enumValues.description: Feed is checked every 2 hours for new updates

enumValues.name: MEDIUM
enumValues.description: Feed is checked every 6 hours for new updates

enumValues.name: REGULAR
enumValues.description: Feed is checked once a day for new updates

enumValues.name: INACTIVE
enumValues.description: Feed is checked once a week for new updates

enumValues.name: LOW
enumValues.description: Feed is checked once a month for new updates

enumValues.name: NEVER
enumValues.description: Feed is no longer checked for updates

types.name: FeedRefreshPriorityReason
types.description: The different types of reasons why a feed has a LOW, INACTIVE, or NEVER priority

enumValues.name: INACTIVE_FOR_OVER_1_YEAR
enumValues.description: Feed has not had any updates in the last 12 months

enumValues.name: DUPLICATE_FEED
enumValues.description: There is another feed in our database that links to the same content

enumValues.name: ERROR_PARSING_FEED
enumValues.description: Error parsing document when trying to check the feed for new updates

enumValues.name: FEED_URL_NOT_WORKING
enumValues.description: Error when trying to load the feed url (404 error, etc)

types.name: Query
types.description: Please go to https://taddy.org/developers for full documentation of the API

fields.name: getPodcastSeries
fields.description: Get details on a Podcast

args.name: uuid
args.description: Get a podcast by its unique identifier (uuid)

args.name: itunesId
args.description: Get a podcast by its itunesId

args.name: rssUrl
args.description: Get a podcast by its RSS feed url

args.name: name
args.description: The name (title) of a podcast. Note: Multiple podcasts can have the exact same name, in that case we always try to return the most popular podcast (based on infomation we have on the podcast popularity)

fields.name: getMultiplePodcastSeries
fields.description: Get details on multiple podcasts

args.name: uuids
args.description: An Array of taddy's unique identifier (uuid). Max 25 IDs allowed

fields.name: getPodcastEpisode
fields.description: Get details on a podcast episode

args.name: uuid
args.description: Taddy's unique identifier (uuid)

args.name: guid
args.description: An episode's guid (from its RSS Feed). Note: guid is not guaranteed to be unique (two different podcasts can use the same guid for two different episodes). If you know the podcast series uuid, you can use seriesUuidForLookup to filter the results to only return episodes for that series

args.name: name
args.description: The name (title) of a episode. Note: Multiple episodes can have the exact same name. If you know the podcast series uuid, you can use seriesUuidForLookup to filter the results to only return episodes for that series

args.name: seriesUuidForLookup
args.description: If you know the podcast series uuid, you can use this to filter the results to only return episodes for that series. This is useful if you are if you using this query and passing in guid or name and want to ensure you get the correct episode

fields.name: getMultiplePodcastEpisodes
fields.description: Get details on multiple podcast episodes

args.name: uuids
args.description: An Array of taddy's unique identifier (uuid). Max 25 IDs allowed

args.name: uuid
args.description: Taddy's unique identifier (uuid) for the episode

args.name: style
args.description: (Optional) Style option for transcript. Default is PARAGRAPH

args.name: useOnDemandCreditsIfNeeded
args.description: (Optional) If the transcript is not available, use on-demand transcription. Default is true. On-demand transcription takes ~10 seconds for every 1 hour of audio transcribed.

args.name: uuid
args.description: Taddy's unique identifier (uuid) for the episode

fields.name: getLatestPodcastEpisodes
fields.description: Get the newly released episodes for a list of podcasts

args.name: uuids
args.description: An Array of taddy's unique identifier (uuid). Max 1000 uuids allowed

args.name: rssUrls
args.description: An Array of urls to the RSS feeds for the podcasts. Max 1000 rssUrls allowed

args.name: page
args.description: Allows for pagination. The page number to return. Default is 1. Max value is 20

args.name: limitPerPage
args.description: Allows for pagination. The number of episodes to return per page. Default is 25. Max value is 50

args.description: Get a comic by its unique identifier (uuid)

args.name: sssUrl

args.name: name
args.description: Get a comic by its name

args.name: uuids
args.description: An Array of taddy's unique identifier (uuid). Max 25 IDs allowed

fields.name: searchForTerm
fields.description: Get Search results details for a term

args.name: term
args.description: (Required) The term you are searching for

args.name: page
args.description: (Optional) Allows for pagination. Default is 1 (ie: page 1 of the results). Max value is 20.

args.name: limitPerPage
args.description: (Optional) The number of results per page. Default is 10. Max value is 25 (ie: that max results you can return in one query in 25)

args.name: sortByDatePublished
args.description: (Optional) Sort results by date content was published. Default is to ignore date but match search by terms passed in. Possible values are LATEST or OLDEST.

args.name: filterForTypes
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only certain content types

args.name: filterForCountries
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content made in certain countries

args.name: filterForLanguages
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content made in certain languages

args.name: filterForGenres
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content from certain genres

args.name: filterForTags
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content that includes a certain tag

args.name: filterForContentRatings
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content that has a certain content rating. For PODCASTSERIES & PODCASTEPISODE use isSafeMode

args.name: filterForContentRoles
args.description: (Optional - only when type is CREATOR) Filter for only creators with a specific role in creating the content

args.name: filterForSeriesUuids
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results only from certain series

args.name: filterForNotInSeriesUuids
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are not from certain series

args.name: filterForPublishedAfter
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are published after a certain date (Epoch time in seconds)

args.name: filterForPublishedBefore
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are published before a certain date (Epoch time in seconds)

args.name: includeSearchOperator
args.description: (Optional) If you want to search for the exact phrase use EXACT_PHRASE. If you want to search for content that contain all terms passed in use AND. If you want to search for content that contain any of the terms passed in use OR. The Default operator is AND.

args.name: isSafeMode
args.description: (Optional - only when type is PODCASTSERIES or PODCASTEPISODE) Choose to only return safe (not explicit) content or all content. Default is false (include everything, including explicit content)

args.name: searchResultsBoostType
args.description: (Optional) By default your search will use BOOST_POPULARITY_A_LITTLE. It will score the results mainly by matching terms but will slightly weight content that is more popular higher. If you care more about getting back popular content than getting an exact match choose BOOST_POPULARITY_A_LOT. If you care about getting an exact match choose BOOST_EXACT_PHRASE.

fields.name: search
fields.description: Get Search results details for a term

args.name: term
args.description: The term you are searching for

args.name: page
args.description: (Optional) Allows for pagination. Default is 1 (ie: page 1 of the results). Max value is 20.

args.name: limitPerPage
args.description: (Optional) The number of results per page. Default is 10. Max value is 25 (ie: that max results you can return in one query in 25)

args.name: filterForTypes
args.description: (Optional) Filter for certain types of content. Default is PODCASTSERIES. Possible values are PODCASTSERIES, PODCASTEPISODE, COMICSERIES, CREATOR

args.name: filterForCountries
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content made in certain countries

args.name: filterForLanguages
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content made in certain languages

args.name: filterForGenres
args.description: (Optional) Filter for only content from certain genres

args.name: filterForSeriesUuids
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results only from certain series

args.name: filterForNotInSeriesUuids
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are not from certain series

args.name: filterForPublishedAfter
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are published after a certain date (Epoch time in seconds)

args.name: filterForPublishedBefore
args.description: (Optional) Filter for results that are published before a certain date (Epoch time in seconds)

args.name: filterForLastUpdatedAfter
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTSERIES & COMICSERIES) Filter for content that have an episode published after a certain date (Epoch time in seconds). This filter is only for PODCASTSERIES & COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForLastUpdatedBefore
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTSERIES & COMICSERIES) Filter for results that have an episode published before a certain date (Epoch time in seconds). This filter is only for PODCASTSERIES & COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForDurationLessThan
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTEPISODE) Filter for episodes that have a duration less than a certain number of seconds. This filter is only for PODCASTEPISODE and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForDurationGreaterThan
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTEPISODE) Filter for episodes that have a duration greater than a certain number of seconds. This filter is only for PODCASTEPISODE and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForTags
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES & CREATOR) Filter for only content that includes a certain tag. Tags are case sensitive and must be exact matches. This filter is only for COMICSERIES & CREATOR and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForContentRatings
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain content rating. This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other types. For PODCASTSERIES & PODCASTEPISODE use isSafeMode

args.name: filterForContentRoles
args.description: (Optional - for CREATOR) Filter for only creators with a specific role in creating the content. This filter is only for CREATOR and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForPodcastContentType
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTSERIES & PODCASTEPISODE) Filter to return only AUDIO or VIDEO podcasts. Default is null (include both AUDIO & VIDEO podcasts). This filter is only for PODCASTSERIES & PODCASTEPISODE and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForComicSeriesType
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for if a comic's series type is WEBTOON, MANGA, etc. Default is null (include all comic types). This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForComicSeriesLayout
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for if a comic's series layout is VERTICAL_SCROLL_TOP_TO_BOTTOM or PAGE etc. Default is null (include all comic layouts). This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForHasTranscript
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTEPISODE) Filter for episodes that have a transcript available. This filter is only for PODCASTEPISODE and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForTotalEpisodesLessThan
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain total number of episodes. This filter is only for PODCASTSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForTotalEpisodesGreaterThan
args.description: (Optional - for PODCASTSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain total number of episodes. This filter is only for PODCASTSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForTotalIssuesLessThan
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain total number of issues. This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForTotalIssuesGreaterThan
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain total number of issues. This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: filterForExclusiveContent
args.description: (Optional - for COMICSERIES) Filter for only content that has a certain scopes for exclusive content. This filter is only for COMICSERIES and will return an empty array for any other type

args.name: sortBy
args.description: (Optional) Choose how the results are sorted. Default is sort by EXACTNESS. Possible values are EXACTNESS and POPULARITY.

args.name: matchBy
args.description: (Optional) Choose which results are matched as valid search results. Default is MOST_TERMS. Possible values are MOST_TERMS, ALL_TERMS, FREQUENCY. If you search has multiple terms, FREQUENCY gives more weight to the terms that appear less frequently in results

args.name: isSafeMode
args.description: (Optional) Choose to only return safe (not explicit) content or all content. Default is false (include everything, including explicit content)

fields.name: getTopCharts
fields.description: Get Top Charts results details for a given source and taddy type

args.name: taddyType
args.description: The type of content you want to get top charts information on.

args.name: by
args.description: Do you want Top charts by genre or country.

args.name: source
args.description: (Optional) The platform to check for top charts content. Default is APPLE_PODCASTS

args.name: country
args.description: (Optional) Which country do you want the Top Charts for.

args.name: genre
args.description: (Optional) Which genre do you want the Top Charts for.

args.name: page
args.description: (Optional) Allows for pagination. Default is 1 (ie: page 1 of the results). Max value is 20.

args.name: limitPerPage
args.description: (Optional) The number of results per page. Default is 10. Max value is 25 (ie: that max results you can return in one query in 25)

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: cost
directives.description: Directive to override the default cost of a field

directives.name: costFactor
directives.description: Directive to override the default cost factor (list multiplier) of a field

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
